verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Almagreira Lunch
verdict.reason: Almagreira is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.

conversationalSummary.text: Alright Pedro, TODAY at lunch looks like the best bet—Almagreira. I’d check the Surfline cams and MEO Beachcam before you leave, because consistency is a bit “lully” and the wind could easily make it cross-off. With the tide dropping and it being quite exposed, it could get a little punchy even though it’s not a big day. If the cams look noticeably better than expected, go for it; otherwise, skip.

best.bestWind: ideal E/ENE/ESE, usable NE/SE, clean <=22 km/h

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Almagreira looks like a fun-sized north coast window for Rare window, Medium confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 49 kJ, 8 s period, lully 13/100, and wind is not fully clear.  An exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal; this is best for small, clean north-coast days when protected spots look underpowered; 6-9s at low tide can still make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia punchy, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully.
best.shortExplanation: Almagreira is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.

photo.alt: Photo of Rey Cortiço surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

ranked.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a small north coast window for Rare window, Medium confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 37 kJ, 9 s period, lully 5/100, and wind is the main risk.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 6-9s at mid tide can turn Belgas punchy and rippable, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot.
ranked.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

ranked.explanation: Lagide looks like a fun-sized window for Fun progression, Medium confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 61 kJ, 8 s period, lully 27/100, and wind is not fully clear.  A dependable left-hand reef that turns modest swell into real rides; this is best for clean, playful walls when the bigger beaches are too wild or too small; 6-9s at low tide is too shallow and dangerous over the reef shelves.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; 6-9s at low tide is too shallow and dangerous over the reef shelves.
ranked.shortExplanation: Lagide is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide makes the reef a bit less forgiving.
warnings: 6-9s at low tide is too shallow and dangerous over the reef shelves

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

ranked.explanation: Cerro looks like a clean fun-sized window for Too soft, Medium confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 49 kJ, 8 s period, lully 21/100, and good wind at 10 km/h.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind; 6-9s at low tide can still make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia punchy.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; Cerro may feel too soft without more push.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cerro is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: Cerro may feel too soft without more push
